_meta.ruling: ratingV5 = editorial peak-career/era-relative rating per docs/RATING_ANCHORS.md. Set-wide +1 was applied to MID/DEF non-movers; 12 position-movers were re-rated against their destination class (position-moves.json).

ratings.name: Lionel Messi
ratings.note: owner override: the GOAT — the first 95 spent; eight Ballon d'Ors, a World Cup, the most complete attacker the game has seen

ratings.name: Cristiano Ronaldo
ratings.note: owner override: alone at 94 among attackers — five Ballon d'Ors and one of the two defining forwards of his era

ratings.name: Pelé
ratings.note: owner override: all-time great, restructured off the 94 tier — three World Cups and roughly a thousand goals

ratings.name: Diego Maradona
ratings.note: owner override: all-time great, restructured off the 94 tier — carried Argentina to the 1986 World Cup single-handedly

ratings.name: Neymar
ratings.note: One of the finest forwards of his generation — Barça's treble front three, PSG's record signing and Brazil's talisman at his peak

ratings.name: Kylian Mbappé
ratings.note: A generational forward — 2018 World Cup winner, a World Cup final hat-trick, serial Golden Boots; already on an all-time trajectory

ratings.name: Johan Cruyff
ratings.note: owner override: all-time great, restructured off the 94 tier — three Ballon d'Ors and the embodiment of Total Football

ratings.name: Ronaldo Nazário
ratings.note: owner override: all-time great, restructured off the 94 tier — two Ballon d'Ors and 'Il Fenomeno', the most devastating striker at peak

ratings.name: Zlatan Ibrahimović
ratings.note: A prolific, dominant centre-forward across seven top leagues for two decades — at peak elite and unplayable, Sweden's greatest

ratings.name: Ronaldinho
ratings.note: Two-time World Player and the 2005 Ballon d'Or — at his peak the most joyful, unplayable footballer on earth

ratings.name: Luis Suárez
ratings.note: owner override: elevated to 92 — a European Golden Boot machine at Liverpool and Barça's treble front three, an all-time great

ratings.name: Karim Benzema
ratings.note: owner override: elevated to 91 — the 2022 Ballon d'Or, Real Madrid's complete striker and talisman, an all-time great

ratings.name: Robert Lewandowski
ratings.note: A relentless goalscoring machine — the best striker in the world across 2020-21, prolific for Bayern, Barça and Poland

ratings.name: Thierry Henry
ratings.note: Arsenal's greatest ever; at his peak the most complete and unstoppable forward in the world, France's record scorer for years

ratings.name: Wayne Rooney
ratings.note: Peak world-class centre-forward/second striker — England & Man Utd's record scorer; sits with Kane 89 / Drogba 87 in the ATT class

ratings.name: Antoine Griezmann
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Robin van Persie
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Eusébio
ratings.note: Ballon d'Or 1965 and the 1966 World Cup's top scorer; Benfica's Black Panther, one of the greatest forwards of all time

ratings.name: George Weah
ratings.note: The 1995 Ballon d'Or — still the only African winner — and FIFA World Player; a complete forward at his Milan/PSG peak

ratings.name: Thomas Müller
ratings.note: A World Cup Golden Boot 'Raumdeuter' with elite forward output for two Bayern trebles — world-class peak; matches prior 87 vs ATT peers

ratings.name: Ferenc Puskás
ratings.note: One of the greatest goalscorers the game has seen — the Magical Magyars' talisman and a Real Madrid great

ratings.name: Harry Kane
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Eden Hazard
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Lukas Podolski
ratings.note: Germany's thunderous-left-foot forward, prolific for country — very good international level in ATT

ratings.name: Gerd Müller
ratings.note: The greatest pure goalscorer of his era — a 1974 World Cup winner and record scorer, Bayern's Der Bomber

ratings.name: Alfredo Di Stéfano
ratings.note: owner override: all-time great, restructured off the 94 tier — dominated the 1950s with five straight European Cups

ratings.name: Olivier Giroud
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Romelu Lukaku
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Marco van Basten
ratings.note: Three Ballon d'Ors and the greatest centre-forward of his era; a Milan and Netherlands (Euro 88) great before injury cut it short

ratings.name: Diego Forlán
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Ousmane Dembélé
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Romário
ratings.note: The 1994 Ballon d'Or and World Cup winner; one of the greatest penalty-box finishers the game has ever seen

ratings.name: Garrincha
ratings.note: The 1962 World Cup genius and one of the greatest dribblers ever — Brazil's joy incarnate alongside Pelé

ratings.name: Rivaldo
ratings.note: The 1999 Ballon d'Or; a magical left foot who dragged Brazil to the 2002 World Cup and starred in Barça's attack

ratings.name: Riyad Mahrez
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Mario Mandžukić
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Gonzalo Higuaín
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Roberto Firmino
ratings.note: Liverpool's selfless false-9 through a CL/PL peak — top of the very-good band among attackers

ratings.name: Hulk
ratings.note: A powerful, prolific goal-scoring forward/winger (Porto/Zenit/Brazil) — very good in the ATT class

ratings.name: Filippo Inzaghi
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Karl-Heinz Rummenigge
ratings.note: Two Ballon d'Ors and West Germany's talisman; Bayern's prolific forward and captain of the early 1980s

ratings.name: Lautaro Martínez
ratings.note: owner override

ratings.name: Lorenzo Insigne
ratings.note: A gifted left winger/forward (Napoli/Italy, Euro 2020) — very good in the ATT class

ratings.name: Federico Chiesa
ratings.note: A direct winger/forward (Euro 2020, Juve/Italy) — very good international level in ATT

ratings.name: Douglas Costa
ratings.note: An explosive out-and-out winger (Bayern/Juve/Brazil) — solid-to-very-good among attackers
